codeReview.summary: The implementation comprehensively addresses the feature requirements for Node B production-safety. It implements defense-in-depth exposure control (localhost-only binding + firewall), upstream health observability through the proxy, systemd hardening with restart behavior, and thorough test coverage for restart recovery scenarios.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The restart-readiness.md documentation is comprehensive and covers VAL-DEPLOY-007, VAL-DEPLOY-008, and VAL-CROSS-118 in detail with verification commands and test mapping. The nixos-config.md was also updated with hardening notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: New files .factory/library/restart-readiness.md (186 lines) and updates to .factory/library/nixos-config.md (29 lines added)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed established patterns for Go service architecture (server package with configurable binding), proxy health checks with upstream status, and systematic test naming that references validation IDs (e.g., TestProxyHealthReportsOkWhenUpstreamIsHealthy).
The services.yaml proxy entry was updated to include 'depends_on: [auth, sandbox]' which aligns with the NixOS service ordering. This keeps local dev parity with production.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml proxy service definition includes proper depends_on matching the systemd after/wants/requires configuration in nix/node-b.nix

summary: This feature implementation successfully hardens Node B for deploy-readiness with defense-in-depth exposure control (localhost-only binding), comprehensive restart-safe behavior, and upstream health observability. The code is well-structured, thoroughly tested (11 new tests across auth, proxy, and server packages), and properly documented. The worker followed the infra-worker skill procedure correctly and left clear documentation for remaining Node B verification steps. All Go tests pass and the Nix flake evaluates successfully.
